title: Possible SQL Injection
description: Unsanitized input from parameter id reaches raw SQL query in app/routes/orders.py
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-89 (SQL Injection). Priority score is 0.72, categorized as high. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding SAST-001, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements, validate input, and avoid string concatenation in SQL.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: SAST-only signals cannot trigger WAF rule generation.

title: Hardcoded API key found
description: Secret key token in code inside settings.py
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-798 (Use of Hard-coded Credentials). Priority score is 0.54, categorized as medium. Reachability is not confirmed, so exploitation likelihood is reduced. This appears similar to existing finding SAST-002, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Remove secrets from source code, rotate exposed credentials, and use a secret manager.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: SAST-only signals cannot trigger WAF rule generation.

title: Reflected XSS sink
description: User controlled value is written through innerHTML in web/src/Search.tsx
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-79 (Cross-Site Scripting). Priority score is 0.58, categorized as medium. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding SAST-003, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Encode output by context, sanitize untrusted HTML, and use safe templating APIs.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: SAST-only signals cannot trigger WAF rule generation.

title: SQL Injection
description: SQL error detected from parameter id on /api/orders using payload ' OR 1=1 --
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-89 (SQL Injection). Priority score is 0.75, categorized as high. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding DAST-001, so deduplication review is recommended. A WAF virtual patch proposal can be reviewed by a human approver.
recommended_fix: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements, validate input, and avoid string concatenation in SQL.
proposed_waf_rule: SecRule ARGS:id "(?i:(union\s+select|select\s+.*from|or\s+1=1))" "id:100001,phase:2,deny,status:403,msg:'Proposed SQLi virtual patch for /api/orders'"
safety_notes: WAF rule is only a proposal and requires human approval before deployment.

title: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting
reachability_reason: DAST finding implies endpoint was dynamically reachable
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-79 (Cross-Site Scripting). Priority score is 0.64, categorized as medium. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding DAST-002, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Encode output by context, sanitize untrusted HTML, and use safe templating APIs.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: priority score below WAF proposal threshold 0.75.

title: Vulnerable dependency log4j-core
description: Dependency package log4j-core version 2.14.1 is affected by remote code execution and unsafe lookup behavior
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-20 (Improper Input Validation). Priority score is 0.65, categorized as medium. Reachability is not confirmed, so exploitation likelihood is reduced. This appears similar to existing finding SCA-001, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Validate, normalize, and constrain all untrusted input at trust boundaries.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: CWE-20 is not eligible for generic WAF virtual patching in this MVP.

title: Package vulnerable to path traversal
description: Package file-utils version 1.3.0 has arbitrary file read through path traversal ../ sequence
reachability_reason: SCA package finding requires dependency reachability confirmation
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-22 (Path Traversal). Priority score is 0.56, categorized as medium. Reachability is not confirmed, so exploitation likelihood is reduced. This appears similar to existing finding SCA-002, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Normalize and constrain file paths, use allowlists, and block traversal sequences.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: finding is not confirmed reachable.
